name: Olympics
description: Olympic Games history, athletes, and records

questions.q: Who was the first gymnast in Olympic history to be awarded a perfect 10?

source.notes: Romanian gymnast, age 14, at the 1976 Montreal Olympics. Scoreboards only went to 9.99.

questions.q: In what year were the first Winter Olympic Games held?

source.notes: Held in Chamonix, France. Originally called 'International Winter Sports Week.'

questions.q: Which country has won the most medals in the history of the Winter Olympics?

source.notes: Dominant in cross-country skiing, biathlon, and speed skating.

questions.q: Who holds the record for the most Olympic medals ever won by a single athlete?

source.notes: 28 total medals (23 gold). Retired after 2016 Rio Olympics.

questions.q: How long is a standard Olympic swimming pool?

source.notes: Also called 'long course.' Short course pools are 25m.

questions.q: Who lit the Olympic cauldron at the 1996 Atlanta Summer Games?

source.notes: Surprise moment; visibly shaking from Parkinson's disease.

questions.q: How many events make up the Olympic decathlon?

source.notes: From Greek 'deca' (10). Heptathlon (for women originally) has 7 events.

questions.q: At which Olympics did Jesse Owens win four gold medals, famously in front of Adolf Hitler?

source.notes: 100m, 200m, long jump, 4x100m relay.

questions.q: Which U.S. city hosted the 1984 Summer Olympics?

source.notes: Boycotted by USSR and 13 Eastern Bloc countries in retaliation for US 1980 boycott.

questions.q: What is the color of the center ring on the Olympic flag?

source.notes: 5 rings (blue, yellow, black, green, red) represent the 5 inhabited continents.

questions.q: In which year were the first Olympic mascots introduced?

source.notes: Munich Games. 'Waldi' the dachshund was the first mascot.

questions.q: Which sport returned to the Olympics in 2016 after a 112-year absence?

source.notes: Last appeared in 1904 St. Louis. Rugby also returned (as sevens) in 2016 after 1924.

questions.q: Tokyo hosted the Summer Olympics for the first time in which year?

source.notes: First Olympics in Asia. Originally awarded the 1940 Games, cancelled by WWII. Tokyo hosted again in 2021.

questions.q: The highest honor for Olympic sportsmanship, the Coubertin Medal, is named after which founder?

source.notes: French aristocrat who revived the modern Olympic Games in 1896.

questions.q: In relay races, what is the common name for the final runner?

source.notes: Typically the fastest runner on the team. Runs the last leg.

questions.q: Who became the first African American woman to win an Olympic gold medal (1948, high jump)?

source.notes: Won gold at the 1948 London Games. Only American woman to win gold in those games.

questions.q: Eddie Eagan is uniquely the only person to have won gold medals in which two different Olympics?
choices: Two different sports at the same Games

source.notes: Boxing (1920 Antwerp) and 4-man bobsled (1932 Lake Placid).

questions.q: Which city hosted the 2024 Summer Olympics?

source.notes: Third time Paris hosted (1900, 1924, 2024). LA will host in 2028.

questions.q: Which team sport was the first added to the modern Olympic Games, in 1900?

source.notes: Paris 1900. Also introduced cricket, rugby, and water polo that year.

questions.q: Michael Jordan won gold medals in which Olympic sport in 1984 and 1992?

source.notes: 1992 Barcelona team known as the 'Dream Team' — first to include NBA pros.
